serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of bird's nest soup is approximately 200 g (one bowl or cup), as typically served in Singapore and dessert shops.

nutrient_source.title: Edible Bird's Nest (Swiftlet Nest) — Nutrient Composition; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entry for bird's nest and peer-reviewed compositional studies (Marcone 2005, Food Research International)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) and peer-reviewed literature (Marcone MF, Food Research International, 2005)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for prepared bird's nest soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a ready-to-eat dish. Values are adapted from published compositional data for edible bird's nest (Marcone 2005, Food Research International 38:1125–1134) combined with dilution adjustment for the prepared soup form (approximately 1–2 g dry nest per 200 mL water with rock sugar). Per-100g values reflect the prepared soup as consumed, not the dry nest ingredient.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimation for bird's nest soup based on analogous low-GI protein-carbohydrate beverage; reference: University of Sydney GI Database — sugar-sweetened low-protein beverages and gelatinous food analogues
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for bird's nest soup exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI is estimated at approximately 40 (low) based on analogy with lightly sweetened gelatinous protein-containing beverages and dilute sugar solutions. The primary carbohydrate source is rock sugar (sucrose, GI ~65) but is highly diluted in the prepared soup; the glycoprotein content of bird's nest may further moderate glycemic response. GL per 200 g serving = (40 × 3.5 × 2) / 100 = 2.8. This is a conservative analogous estimate; direct GI testing is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~10 mg per 100 g in prepared soup form)
hypertension.guidance: Bird's nest soup is very low in sodium (~20 mg per 200 g serving) and poses minimal concern for individuals managing hypertension, provided it is prepared without added salt or high-sodium condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic load and modest sugar content from diluted rock sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: Bird's nest soup has a low estimated glycemic index (~40) and a low glycemic load per serving (~2.8), making it a relatively safe occasional treat for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, though portion size and added sugar should be monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; low caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Bird's nest soup contains negligible saturated fat and cholesterol and is very low in calories (~36 kcal per 200 g serving), making it a heart-friendly food choice when consumed without excessive added sugar.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bird's nest soup (燕窝汤, Yàn wō tāng) is a traditional Chinese tonic beverage and dessert soup made from the edible nest of swiftlets (genus Aerodramus), which construct their nests primarily from hardened saliva. The nests are harvested, cleaned, and double-boiled with water and rock sugar to produce a lightly sweet, gelatinous soup with a delicate, subtle flavour and a soft, slightly chewy texture. In Singapore, bird's nest soup is widely consumed as a health tonic, dessert, and gift item, particularly within the Chinese community. It is available in both warm and chilled forms and is sold at traditional Chinese dessert shops, high-end restaurants, and specialty health-food retailers. The dish is prized in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported benefits to skin health, immunity, and overall vitality, attributed to its glycoprotein content including sialic acid. Nutritionally, the prepared soup is very low in calories (approximately 18 kcal per 100 g), contains a small amount of protein (~0.9 g per 100 g) derived from the nest's glycoprotein matrix, minimal fat, and a modest carbohydrate content (~3.5 g per 100 g) primarily from added rock sugar. It has a low estimated glycemic index (~40) and a low glycemic load per serving, making it suitable for most dietary patterns. The sodium content is very low. Bird's nest soup is gluten-free and dairy-free. It is not suitable for vegans or vegetarians as it is an animal-derived product. Clinically, it poses low concern for hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease when consumed in standard portions without excessive added sugar.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for prepared bird's nest soup exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central as a ready-to-eat dish. Nutrient values are adapted from peer-reviewed compositional data for edible bird's nest (Marcone 2005) with dilution adjustment for the prepared soup form. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the compositional literature for bird's nest is well-established and the dilution model is defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values reflect the prepared soup as consumed (approximately 1–2 g dry nest per 200 mL water with rock sugar), not the dry nest ingredient. Per-100g values are adapted from Marcone MF (2005), Food Research International 38:1125–1134, with dilution modelling. 2. saturated_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — bird's nest is an animal glycoprotein product with negligible lipid content; the prepared soup contains no meaningful saturated fat per the compositional literature. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — no trans fats are present in this food by nature or preparation. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per compositional literature; bird's nest glycoproteins do not contribute dietary cholesterol. 5. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — bird's nest and rock sugar contain no dietary fibre. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature for prepared bird's nest soup. 7. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature for prepared bird's nest soup. 8. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature for prepared bird's nest soup. 9. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature for prepared bird's nest soup. 10. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature. 11. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source compositional literature. 12. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 13. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 14. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 15. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 16. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 17. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. 18. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 19. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 20. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 21. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 22. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. 23. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 24. GI is an analogous estimate (approximately 40, low category) as no direct GI measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for bird's nest soup; direct GI testing is recommended. 25. The input record listed the food name as 'Bird's nest soup 燕窝水' — 燕窝水 (yàn wō shuǐ) refers to the liquid/drink form; the canonical English name is standardised to 'Bird's nest soup' and both 燕窝汤 (soup) and 燕窝水 (drink/water form) are captured in aliases. 26. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes use closest_parent match type as no exact prepared-soup codes exist. 27. Served at Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road on field survey data at SGD 11.90.
